name: Coupon usage increased (mixed result)
description: Documented Coupon Usage response wrapping a couponUsageResultList. First coupon succeeds with remaining redemption counts; second fails because the code does not exist (both entries taken verbatim from the doc's Coupon Usage Result sample response).
notes: Verified against the official guide. Resource definition at lines 24004-24064; response schema "Coupon Usage" + "Coupon Usage Result" at lines 28918-28981. CAVEAT on the response wrapper: the "Coupon Usage" response body (line 28918) documents a single property whose JSON name is rendered as "coupon UsageResult List" of type "Coupon Usage Result[]" — the PDF-to-text extraction wraps the camelCase property name across lines. I reconstructed it as `couponUsageResultList`; the exact on-the-wire key could not be confirmed from an inline JSON example of the wrapper itself (the doc only shows the inner array sample under "Coupon Usage Result"). Implementers should confirm the wrapper key casing against a live org response. Request properties (all Required, v62.0): couponCodeList (List<String>), activityDateTime (DateTime), externalTransactionNumber (String), customerId (String). Doc request example used unpadded ids ("0lMxx0000000001"); I padded customerId to a realistic 18-char Salesforce id (0lMxx0000000001AAA) per task instructions. Available version of the resource is 63.0 though individual fields show 62.0. No separate error/empty top-level response is documented — the error case is represented per-coupon via isSuccess:false + errorMessage inside the same 200 response, so a single fixture covers both success and failure entries.
